item.title: joined Kawai Lab. as a posdoc.
item.description: I completed my doctoral coarse and joined Kawai Lab. as a posdoc.

item.title: Our new paper 'Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation' has appeared in AIAA Journal
item.description: Our new paper “Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation” which I contributed to as a co-first author has appeared in AIAA Journal! We proposed a sensor that detects turbulent breakdown associated with the rapid increase in skin friction to activate the wall model. The proposed sensor-based wall-model calculation achieves high fidelity simulations of various scenarios of boundary layer transitions subject to wall heating/cooling with significantly reduced cost compared to the DNS!

item.title: Our new paper 'Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated and cooled boundary layers' has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows
item.description: A new Open Access paper “Compact representation of transonic airfoil buffet flows with observable-augmented machine learning” led by Associate Professor K. Fukami (Tohoku Univ., Japan) has appeared in Journal of Fluid Mechanics!

item.title: Our new paper 'Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated and cooled boundary layers' has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows
item.description: Our new Open Access paper “Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated, and cooled boundary layers” has appeared in International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flows! In the paper, it was found from the DNS data obtained using Fugaku supercomputer that, at a specific condition, wall cooling can alter the transition scenario from H-type to O-type by suppressing the first (TS) mode’s growth. Owing to the shift of transition scenario, the turbulent breakdown is delayed drastically. The [DNS database] presented in this paper will be available soon! (will be uploaded “here”)

item.title: Creating a webpage with Hugo & Publishing it with Github Pages.

item.description:
Publications

In Review

2026

  • Kawai, S., Iwatani, Y., Eto, C., and Kawai, S., “Detecting Turbulent Breakdown for Predicting Transitional Boundary Layers in Wall-Modeled Large-Eddy Simulation,” AIAA Journal, 2026, pp. 1–9. https://doi.org/10.2514/1.j066244 (co-first)

2025

  • K. Fukami, Y. Iwatani, S. Maejima, H. Asada, and S. Kawai, “Compact representation of transonic airfoil buffet flows with observable-augmented machine learning”, Journal of Fluid Mechanics, vol. 1021, p. A39, 2025. doi:10.1017/jfm.2025.10741

  • Y. Iwatani, H. Asada, and S. Kawai, “Direct numerical simulation of subharmonic transition in pseudo-adiabatic, heated and cooled boundary layers”, International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flow, 2025, Volume 116, December 2025, 109896.

item.title: Yuta Iwatani's C.V.
item.description:
Yuta Iwatani

  • Master of Engineering, 2023
  • JSPS Research Fellowship for Young Scientists, DC1, 2023~

Education

  • M.S. (Eng.) Tohoku University, Japan 2023 (supervisor: Professor Soshi Kawai)
  • B.S. (Eng.) Tohoku University, Japan 2021 (supervisor: Professor Soshi Kawai)

Awards

2021 第35回数値流体力学シンポジウム 若手優秀講演表彰 [link]
